title: Sangre De Cristo Midnight V2
description: Black sky pressing down from above, the mountains built from flat planes of yellow, magenta, hot pink, and navy that stack like geological strata reimagined as a screen-print. The circuit board rising from the valley is no longer subtle — it's multicolor, green and orange-red, transmitting at full strength into the dark. Part of the Sangre de Cristos series — nine variations of the same Colorado mountain range, each a different emotional frequency.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Midnight V2 — dark digital landscape of the Sangre de Cristo mountain range under a pure black star-dotted sky. Mountain forms rendered in bold blocks of yellow, magenta, hot pink, and navy blue with neon green contour line detailing. A multicolor circuit board trace pattern in green and orange-red rises dramatically from the central valley. Part of the BasicGlitch Sangre de Cristos series.

title: Sangre De Cristo Dusk
description: The quietest piece in the Sangre de Cristos series. Peach sky, pale lavender snow, circuit traces in soft white — the mountains gentled down to pastel. The same data, the same geology, run through an entirely different emotional state. Where Midnight is aggressive and Neon is electric, Dusk is the version you'd describe to someone who asked what it feels like to watch the light change on a high peak.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Dusk — soft pastel digital landscape of the Sangre de Cristo mountain range under a warm peach and amber gradient sky. Mountain peaks and snowfields rendered in pale cyan and soft white. Lower slopes and foreground in muted lavender and blush pink. Delicate white circuit board trace pattern rises from the central valley. Part of the BasicGlitch Sangre de Cristos series.

title: Screambot
description: Munch painted existential dread. BasicGlitch gave it a vinyl player in its chest and a cooling fan where its mouth should be. Broboticus stands on the bridge while the sky swirls orange and blue and the two figures behind him mind their business, utterly unaware of the frequency he's receiving. He is not having a breakdown. He is receiving too many frequencies simultaneously and has not yet found the right filter.
alt_text: Screambot — comic art style digital painting of Broboticus robot in the pose of Edvard Munch's The Scream, hands raised to the sides of his head, standing on a bridge. Blue cooling-fan eyes leak cyan tears. His chest panel houses a vinyl record player and analog gauges. The background recreates The Scream's swirling orange and blue sky with two dark figures on the bridge behind him. BasicGlitch Masters Remixed series.

title: Marilyn Monbroe
description: Warhol's Marilyn series is about the mechanical reproduction of an icon. BasicGlitch takes that logic one step further: replace the face with Broboticus in a pleated skirt throwing double rock horns, and alternate with panels of oversized multicolor lips that could belong to either of them. Six panels across blue, yellow, lime green, lavender, purple, and red. Warhol's palette, Warhol's format, Warhol's argument — except the face is a BROBOTICUS chest panel. The title is perfect.
alt_text: Marilyn Monbroe — Warhol-style pop art grid of six panels in blue, yellow, lime green, lavender, purple, and red. Three panels show Broboticus wearing a pleated skirt throwing rock horns, chest panel labeled BROBOTICUS. Three alternating panels show large multicolor lips in rainbow tones. BasicGlitch Masters Remixed series.

title: Psylent Skulls
description: Motion art by BasicGlitch. Skulls and surreal forms shifting through digital space — the glitch as a living thing rather than an artifact.
alt_text: Psylent Skulls — motion art video by BasicGlitch featuring surreal skull forms in digital transformation.

title: Mountain Soul
description: Motion art by BasicGlitch. The Sangre de Cristo landscape in motion — the circuit board and the mountain range as living, breathing signal.
alt_text: Mountain Soul — motion art video by BasicGlitch featuring the Sangre de Cristo mountain landscape with circuit board animation.

title: BasicGlitch Neon Reel
description: High-resolution motion reel showcasing the BasicGlitch visual language — neon surrealism, circuit landscapes, and character design in motion.
alt_text: BasicGlitch Neon Reel — high resolution motion art showcase by BasicGlitch featuring neon surrealism and glitch aesthetics.

title: Skyline Reclaimation
description: A cityscape where the organic is reclaiming what was taken. Architecture and root systems entangled, the circuit board and the vine growing toward the same light. BasicGlitch's cyber-eclectic vision of what cities become when nature and technology stop pretending to be opposites.
alt_text: Skyline Reclaimation — cyber-eclectic digital landscape showing urban architecture merged with organic growth and circuit patterns. BasicGlitch.

title: Sentinel Stream
description: Something watches from the water's edge where the stream meets the circuit. A tech-noir landscape with the quality of a dream you almost remember — organic textures against synthetic structures, the sentinel presence that mediates between two worlds.
alt_text: Sentinel Stream — cyber-eclectic digital landscape where organic and technological forms merge at a stream. Tech-noir surrealism by BasicGlitch.

title: Monument Memory
description: Classical monuments run through the BasicGlitch filter — columns and pediments rendered semi-transparent as digital artifacts permeate their surfaces. A meditation on what it means to preserve the past in an age that processes everything into data. The stone remains. The signal surrounds it.
alt_text: Monument Memory — cyber-eclectic digital surrealism depicting classical architectural monuments fragmented by digital elements. BasicGlitch.

title: Kinetic Latency
description: The lag made visible. Kinetic Latency captures that specific digital moment when motion and delay collide — the frame that almost resolved but didn't, the image that reached you a half-second behind where it should be. This is what speed looks like when the system can't quite keep up.
alt_text: Kinetic Latency — cyber-eclectic glitch art depicting motion and digital delay. Glitch surrealism by BasicGlitch.

title: Apex Rendering
description: The render at its limit — everything the machine can produce pressed to the edge of coherence. Apex Rendering sits at the boundary between the image completing itself and the image dissolving into its own process. Tech-noir aesthetic pushed to maximum.
alt_text: Apex Rendering — cyber-eclectic glitch art at the boundary of coherence and dissolution. Tech-noir surrealism by BasicGlitch.

title: Pup Fiction — Crash (Diner Robbery Prologue)
description: The prologue to BasicGlitch's Pup Fiction series drops you straight into the chaos. A Chihuahua stands on the diner table, revolver raised, delivering the iconic monologue to a room full of stunned patrons. A Jack Russell in a leather jacket holds his piece on the booth. A bulldog cook and a bewildered elderly Dachshund complete the freeze-frame. The comic book title treatment — blood-drip lettering, yellowed panel borders — makes this feel less like fan art and more like a parallel universe where this story was always told in fur.
alt_text: Pup Fiction Crash — digital art parody of Pulp Fiction's diner robbery scene with dogs as all characters. A Chihuahua brandishes a revolver on a diner table while a Jack Russell holds another gun from a booth. A bulldog cook and elderly Dachshund look on in shock. Comic book title card reads PUP FICTION CRASH. BasicGlitch.

title: Gaia of the Wasteland
description: She is the last living thing and also the land itself. Rooted in cracked earth, crowned in canopy, lit from behind like something that refuses to be extinguished. Gaia doesn't grieve. She endures. The contrast between her vitality and the dead world around her is the piece's whole argument — she is what remains when everything else is gone. Whether that reads as hopeful or devastating depends entirely on what you bring to it. Companion piece to Dust to Dust.
alt_text: Gaia of the Wasteland — surreal digital art of a woman whose body merges with tree roots and bark, standing in a cracked desert landscape. Her natural hair becomes a full tree canopy with a glowing halo of light behind it. A dead tree stands in the background. Environmental surrealism by BasicGlitch.

title: Guitarbot (Brobassicus)
description: Somewhere in the cosmos, Broboticus plugged in and never stopped. Bass frequencies, multicolor starfields, and a beanie that somehow makes total sense on a robot. He was called and he answered. This is the musician chapter of the Case Study 42 archive — the documentation of what happens when a robot discovers rhythm and decides it's the most important thing in the universe. He's not performing. He's transmitting.
alt_text: Guitarbot Brobassicus — neon green robot character with banded beanie head and speaker-chest playing an electric bass guitar against a deep purple cosmic background filled with multicolored stars. BasicGlitch original character digital art. Case Study 42 Broboticus series.

title: Spiral Geometry (Fire Variant)
description: Start at the center. You won't be able to stop. A hand-drawn mandala erupting from a yellow point through red petals and violet webbing, surrounded by a black-and-white geometric field that pulls the eye into the spiral and refuses to release it. This is BasicGlitch working in the oldest visual tradition — the mandala — and making it entirely his own. The fire variant of the Spiral Studies series, where color is applied selectively to the center while the outer field remains structural grey.
alt_text: Spiral Geometry — intricate hand-drawn digital mandala with a ten-pointed star flower at center, petals graduating from yellow core through orange and deep red with black dot detailing and violet lattice inner petals. The surrounding field is dense black-and-white geometric pattern. Part of the BasicGlitch Spiral Studies series.

title: Sangre De Cristos — Neon
description: Colorado's Sangre de Cristo range, rendered in two colors and zero compromise. Pure green on black — the mountain forms reduced to their essential geometry, the circuit board rising from the valley between peaks as traces of light. The original piece in the Sangre de Cristos series. The mountains were always processing something. We just couldn't see the traces until now.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Neon — high-contrast digital landscape of Colorado mountain range in neon green on pure black. A circuit board pattern rises from the valley between peaks. A winding river reflects light at the base. Stars dot the upper darkness. Tech-noir landscape art by BasicGlitch.

title: Floral (Spiral Studies — Dusk Variant)
description: The mandala at its most intimate. A deep violet-to-purple gradient background, the center pulled to the lower left so the composition breathes, petals in blush pink and dusty lavender. The same hand-drawn geometry as Spiral Geometry and Monopattern — but quieter, cropped, approached gently. Like the same song played acoustic. Part of the Spiral Studies series.
alt_text: Floral — Spiral Studies mandala rendered in soft pastel palette, composition shifted to lower-left quadrant. Petals in blush pink with white dot sequences and lavender lattice detailing against a deep violet-to-purple gradient background. Part of the BasicGlitch Spiral Studies series.

title: Brobarticus — The Artist
description: Broboticus picks up a brush and discovers something inconvenient: he's painting himself. A recursive loop in neon green. The machine that makes art about the machine that makes art. Lab coat on, palette loaded, record spinning from the turntable mounted in his head — paint splatters across the coat like evidence. He's been at this a while. Case Study 42, Field Report 08.
alt_text: Brobarticus — neon green line art on black of Broboticus the robot standing at an easel in a paint-splattered lab coat, holding a palette and brush. His turntable/cube head plays a record. On the canvas he is painting a portrait of another robot. BasicGlitch original character digital art. Case Study 42.

title: A Mycological Phenomenon
description: Midnight in a field that doesn't exist on any map. One massive Amanita front and center, the moon watching with sleepy-lidded eyes, the sky a deep embroidered purple, every surface stitched and textured like a fever dream someone decided to frame. The moon in the upper right corner has a face — gold, heavy-lidded, lips painted red — and she is utterly unbothered by what's happening below her. Companion piece to Mycology Yourcology.
alt_text: A Mycological Phenomenon — surreal digital landscape with embroidered textile texture depicting a large red and brown Amanita mushroom dominating the center foreground. Lush green ferns fill the lower frame. A crescent moon with a sleeping face glows gold in the upper right. Deep purple-blue night sky filled with white stars and swirling clouds. BasicGlitch.

title: Sangre De Cristos — Night
description: The Sangre de Cristos series in its original form — pure neon green on absolute black, the mountain forms stripped to their essential geometry, the circuit board rising from the valley into the dark sky. This is the range at the moment just before something happens. The mountains were always processing something. We just couldn't see the traces until now.
alt_text: Sangre De Cristos Night — neon green digital landscape of the Sangre de Cristo mountain range at night. Mountain ridgelines glow neon green on black. A circuit board trace pattern rises from the central valley. A winding river runs through the foreground. Part of the BasicGlitch Sangre de Cristos series.

title: The Bolts and Nutcracker (Balletboticus)
description: Case Study 42, Field Report 07: Broboticus discovers ballet. Two positions, one crackling magenta background, and the kind of commitment to form that suggests he's been practicing since his third firmware update. Two versions of the character occupy the canvas — smaller on the left, larger on the right — both holding a single-leg tree pose with arms raised, fingers spread, completely earnest. Broboticus does not half-commit.
alt_text: The Bolts and Nutcracker — digital art showing two versions of Broboticus the robot in ballet poses against a textured magenta and purple background. Both figures balance in tree pose with arms raised. Bold dark outlines on vivid crackle-pattern background. BasicGlitch original character art. Case Study 42.

title: Iron Chef (Chefboticus)
description: Case Study 42, Field Report 03: Broboticus reports to the kitchen. Rolling pin in one hand, cookie tray in the other, chef's toque slightly askew. His eyes glow. The batter is perfect. Rendered in neon green on black against a deep emerald texture — the same visual language as the Sangre de Cristos series. The kitchen is just another arena, and Broboticus enters it with the same gravity he brings to everything else.
alt_text: Iron Chef Chefboticus — neon green line art on black of Broboticus the robot wearing a chef's hat and standing at a kitchen counter. He holds a rolling pin in one hand and a baking tray of cookies in the other. A mixing bowl with whisk sits on the counter. Glowing green eyes on matte black figure against textured green background. BasicGlitch original character art. Case Study 42.

title: Cubist Chef
description: Chefboticus gets the Picasso treatment — fractured planes, burnt sienna and terracotta, a rolling pin in hand and the New York skyline through the kitchen window. He is unbothered by the Chrysler Building. The dough demands focus. Two visual languages, one kitchen: the robot's form shattered into warm geometric facets while Manhattan remains in a deliberately flatter graphic style behind him.
alt_text: Cubist Chef — cubist-style digital oil painting of a robot chef rendered in fragmented geometric planes of burnt orange, terracotta, and brown. He holds a rolling pin over a flour-dusted counter with mixing bowls and a flour sack. A large window behind him frames a New York City skyline including the Chrysler Building and Empire State Building. BasicGlitch original character art.

title: Dust to Dust
description: Same wasteland. Different figure. Where Gaia stands rooted and sovereign, this one is mid-dissolution — her hair carrying the last green thing into the wind. Two faces of the same loss. The moss on her shoulders, the sun low and orange behind a dead tree, the cracked ground identical to her companion piece — these two works share a world. Shown together they tell a fuller story about what Earth looks like when it remembers it is alive, and what it looks like when it isn't sure.
alt_text: Dust to Dust — surreal digital portrait of a woman whose body and roots emerge from cracked desert earth. Tree branches and green foliage grow through her flowing hair, which blows in the wind. Moss patches mark her shoulders. A dead tree and hazy sun appear in the background. Environmental surrealism companion piece to Gaia of the Wasteland by BasicGlitch.

title: Night-Fishing the Arkansas River
description: Case Study 42, Field Report 11: Broboticus fishes the Arkansas at midnight. Striped blue sky, crescent moon, red-glowing eyes fixed on the line. He has been standing here for six hours. He is not leaving until something bites. The horizontal banding of the background gives the whole scene the feel of a CRT screen tuned to somewhere peaceful. The field report simply notes: Broboticus finds the water calming. No further data recorded.
alt_text: Night-Fishing the Arkansas River — retro digital art of Broboticus robot standing at the water's edge holding a fishing rod at night. Rendered in cream line art on black against a horizontally striped blue and purple sky. A yellow crescent moon sits low on the left. His eyes glow red. BasicGlitch original character art. Case Study 42.

title: Karateboticus
description: Case Study 42, Field Report 05: Three Broboticus instances, three stances, zero hesitation. Red, orange, yellow — the field report doesn't specify who threw the first punch, only that the dojo is now a crime scene of excellent technique. Three instances of Broboticus stacked in depth and motion across a cyan splatter field — the composition reads like a stop-motion sequence caught mid-explosion.
alt_text: Karateboticus — pop art digital illustration of three overlapping Broboticus robot figures in dynamic martial arts poses against a cyan blue background with black, blue, and gray paint splatter. Figures outlined in red, orange, and yellow respectively. BasicGlitch original character art. Case Study 42.

title: Literal Traphouse
description: It is exactly what it says. The word TRAP is built into the architecture, neon green on black, integrated into the roofline like a load-bearing pun. The garage door is real. The chimney is real. The wordplay is the foundation. This is the funniest piece in the BasicGlitch archive, and it's funny because it's executed with complete seriousness — single-weight neon green line on black, with the precision of an architectural blueprint.
alt_text: Literal Traphouse — minimalist neon green line art on pure black of a suburban house silhouette with the letters T-R-A-P integrated directly into the structure of the facade. The T forms the left wall, R curves through the center beneath a dormer window, A forms an interior peak, P anchors the right side beside a garage door. BasicGlitch conceptual art.

title: Mycology Yourcology
description: Where A Mycological Phenomenon presents its mushroom as a singular, quietly powerful presence, Mycology Yourcology turns the dial to a different frequency. The sky here is all motion — deep blue and purple swirling in Van Gogh spirals, as if the atmosphere itself is responding to something the mushroom is transmitting. The cap of the Amanita wears spiral whorl patterns now — the mushroom grew eyes while you weren't looking. The two pieces share a world. This one knows you're in it.
alt_text: Mycology Yourcology — surreal embroidered-texture digital landscape featuring a large red Amanita mushroom with spiral whorl patterns on its cap, set against a dramatically swirling blue and purple night sky. Snow-capped mountain peaks flank the background. Lush spiraling green ferns fill the foreground. Companion piece to A Mycological Phenomenon by BasicGlitch.

title: Pachydermis
description: Scale is the argument here. The elephant fills two-thirds of the canvas — face-on, tusks forward, ears spread wide, its hide an impossible blue-gold with mandala patterns orbiting its eye. Behind it, billowing upward in every direction, a cloud formation that has abandoned any pretense of being weather: magenta, crimson, electric blue, toxic green, all folding into fractal floral mandalas at the crown. And at the lower right, barely a silhouette — a lone figure in a long coat, facing all of it. They are not running. The most maximalist piece in the gallery.
alt_text: Pachydermis — high-saturation psychedelic digital surrealism depicting a massive frontal-facing elephant rendered in blue and yellow with ornate mandala-like eye detailing, emerging from an enormous explosion of chromatic clouds in magenta, red, orange, blue, and green. A small lone human silhouette in a long coat stands at the lower right. BasicGlitch.

title: Rancher Broboticus
description: Case Study 42, Field Report 09: Broboticus acquires a horse. The city skyline glows behind them. His chest panel reads BROBOTICUS. His hat is tiny and perfect. He has fully committed to the bit. One fist raised in the air with the casual confidence of someone who has been ranching for at minimum three firmware updates. The field report confirms: yes, Broboticus can ride.
alt_text: Rancher Broboticus — neon green line art on black of Broboticus the robot riding a horse through a city at night. His chest panel is labeled BROBOTICUS with mixer faders and dials. He wears a small cowboy hat and raises one fist while holding reins. A glowing neon city skyline is visible in the background. BasicGlitch original character art. Case Study 42.

title: There's a Star-Bot Waiting in the Sky
description: Bowie said there was a Starman. BasicGlitch rendered his wiring diagram. An extreme close-up wireframe portrait of Broboticus dissolving upward into a multicolor cosmos — green to yellow to pink to red — surrounded by a scatter of chromatic stars. No fills, no solids, just the wireframe skeleton viewed from below in extreme close-up, every panel and joint mapped in layered neon lines. He's been up there this whole time.
alt_text: There's a Star-Bot Waiting in the Sky — extreme close-up low-angle wireframe portrait of Broboticus robot head and upper body rendered in dense neon rainbow line art graduating from green through yellow, orange, and pink against a pure black background scattered with multicolor stars. BasicGlitch original character art.

title: Vitruvian Broboticus
description: Leonardo mapped the ideal human form. BasicGlitch maps the ideal robot — and he's holding a vinyl record in one hand and a pair of headphones in the other. The proportions check out. Arms spread within the circle, legs in the split stance, the whole figure rendered in ink on aged parchment with handwritten annotations in the margins. Where Da Vinci's man is empty-handed, Broboticus holds a vinyl record on one side and headphones on the other. This is not parody. It's a parallel thesis.
alt_text: Vitruvian Broboticus — pen-and-ink style digital art on aged parchment background depicting Broboticus the robot in the pose of Da Vinci's Vitruvian Man, arms outstretched within a circle and square. He holds a vinyl record in his left hand and headphones in his right. Handwritten notes surround the figure in the style of Renaissance scientific illustration. BasicGlitch original character art.

title: Brobotosaurus Wrex
description: Every case study has an origin, and this is Broboticus at full amplitude — rendered in full comic book color rather than signature neon line work. He's astride a saddled T-Rex, reins in one hand, a vintage coiled-cord microphone raised in the other like he's calling the whole thing into the record. Flying saucers trail smoke across a burning orange and purple skyline. Brobotosaurus Wrex is the piece that establishes the scale of this character — not a robot doing leisure activities, but a robot whose leisure activities happen to involve dinosaurs, spacecraft, and the fall of civilization.
alt_text: Brobotosaurus Wrex — full color comic book style digital illustration of a retro robot with red glowing eyes and headphones riding a saddled Tyrannosaurus Rex through a futuristic city at sunset. The robot holds a vintage coiled-cord microphone aloft. Flying saucers streak across an orange and purple sky above skyscrapers. BasicGlitch original character art. Case Study 42 Field Report 00.
